content.problemStatement:
Write a query to output the following
- Month-wise revenue and loss for the complete year 2020
- Order by decreasing profits for each month
- Rename the monthwise revenue column as 'monthly_revenue' and montwise profit / loss column as 'monthly_pnl' while running the query
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Month в”Ӯ monthly_revenue в”Ӯ monthly_pnl в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ Dec   в”Ӯ 884000          в”Ӯ 144000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Feb   в”Ӯ 828750          в”Ӯ 135000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Nov   в”Ӯ 773500          в”Ӯ 126000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Oct   в”Ӯ 663000          в”Ӯ 108000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Sep   в”Ӯ 552500          в”Ӯ 90000       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Jan   в”Ӯ 552500          в”Ӯ 90000       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Jun   в”Ӯ 132600          в”Ӯ 21600       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Jul   в”Ӯ 132600          в”Ӯ 21600       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Aug   в”Ӯ 132600          в”Ӯ 21600       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ May   в”Ӯ 165750          в”Ӯ -36000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Mar   в”Ӯ 165750          в”Ӯ -36000      в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Apr   в”Ӯ 165750          в”Ӯ -36000      в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### Table structure
Table name - Financials
- Month - Text
- Revenue - Integer
- Profit_loss - Integer
- Division - Text
- Product_type - Text
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ вҖ” the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
